page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-80 is a memory-optimized server instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 80 dedicated vCPUs derived from 40 physical Intel Xeon cores running at a base frequency of 2.8 GHz, with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 640.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core processing and memory subsystem capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier results in multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 4060.83 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 145.01 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations are also highly rated, with Redis handling 74.39 million operations per second. However, single-core performance and cryptographic execution fall into the average tier, and LLM text generation is weak, showing a clear tradeoff for sequential generative tasks.

Given the high memory density and dedicated CPU allocation, this instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring large in-memory datasets without the overhead of GPU accelerators. The lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment, which should be factored into architectural planning. The hardware profile is suited for large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, software compilation pipelines, and high-throughput static web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-80 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 80 dedicated vCPUs from 40 physical Intel Xeon cores at 2.8 GHz, paired with 640.0 GB of RAM. Lacking integrated GPUs and local storage, it relies on external storage attachments. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU execution, high memory bandwidth (up to 4060.83 GB/sec cached read), and excellent database performance, including 74.39 million Redis SET operations per second. Conversely, single-core performance is average, and LLM text generation is weak. This profile offers qualitative resource efficiency for memory-intensive applications. It is suited for database hosting, software compilation, and in-memory caching.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-80 is an x86_64 server with 80 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 640 GB RAM. It delivers strong multi-core and memory bandwidth performance for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-80 features 80 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 640 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive databases and multi-core workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 server with eighty dedicated vCPUs and 640 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive databases and high-bandwidth multi-core workloads.

640.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Strong multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark results
No integrated GPU or local storage included in the base configuration
Suited for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and software compilation
